Title: A combined genetic and physical map reveals that genes and recombination events are concentrated near chromosome ends

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: FN and PK; heterologous enzyme expression in Pichia pastoris X-33 for functional assay
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal oxidocyclase branch of cannabinoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts CBGA to CBCA as a cannabinoid oxidocyclase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: May contribute to analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties; discussed as medically relevant cannabinoid
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Secreted protein in heterologous Pichia expression system
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in female floral tissue; minimal in leaf, stem, and root
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in female floral tissue; minimal in leaf, stem, and root
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes CBCA production; CBCA accumulation correlates with CBCAS expression across tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in Pichia pastoris; enzyme assay with CBGA substrate; HPLC product identification; protein purification; kinetic assay; qRT-PCR; tissue metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing and assembly; tissue expression by qRT-PCR; BioProject PRJNA73819
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Enzyme kinetics optimized at 40°C and pH 5.5 for in vitro assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A THCAS-like gene was experimentally shown to encode CBCAS. 2. Recombinant CBCAS converted CBGA to CBCA in Pichia-based assays. 3. CBCAS expression and CBCA accumulation were highest in female flowers, supporting its in planta role in CBCA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for breeding cannabis strains with elevated CBCA content and for engineering novel cannabinoid profiles
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One copy detected in PK assembly and two copies in FN assembly; sequence is highly similar to THCAS and CBDAS, supporting duplication/divergence-driven cannabinoid diversification

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Psychoactive cannabinoid precursor after decarboxylation to THC
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence of a single functional THCAS copy in PK and absence in FN underlies THCA-producing chemotype differences
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome assembly comparison; raw read mapping; genetic mapping of locus on Chromosome 6
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio and Illumina genome data; BioProject PRJNA73819
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PK contains a single functional THCAS copy while FN lacks THCAS. 2. THCAS maps near the known chemotype marker region on Chromosome 6. 3. Structural divergence at the THCAS/CBDAS region contributes to cannabinoid chemotype differences.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: THCAS-containing scaffold in PK also contains a pseudogenic CBDAS copy disrupted by a gypsy insertion; THCAS and CBDAS are at separate loci rather than simple alleles

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Non-intoxicating cannabinoid precursor after decarboxylation to CBD
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence of a single functional CBDAS copy in FN and absence in PK underlies CBDA-producing chemotype differences
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome assembly comparison; raw read mapping; genetic mapping of locus on Chromosome 6
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio and Illumina genome data; BioProject PRJNA73819
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FN contains a single functional CBDAS copy while PK lacks an exact functional CBDAS copy. 2. CBDAS maps near the known chemotype marker region on Chromosome 6. 3. The CBDAS locus is highly rearranged relative to the THCAS-containing region, explaining chemotype divergence.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CBDAS-containing region is embedded within a repeat-rich, gene-poor, nonrecombining ~39 Mb segment; no THCAS equivalent found in hemp FN

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes production of CBGA, the common substrate for THCAS, CBDAS, and CBCAS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive specialized metabolites with medicinal and recreational uses
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Suggested key control point for total cannabinoid production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: PK has >5-fold higher transcript levels than FN
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: PK has >5-fold higher transcript levels than FN
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to total cannabinoid content by controlling CBGA substrate availability
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic linkage to previous QTL marker ANUCS501; comparative transcript level analysis; sequence comparison showing indels
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; comparative transcriptome reference from van Bakel et al. 2011; BioProject PRJNA73819
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AP is linked to a previously reported marker associated with total cannabinoid content. 2. AP produces CBGA, the substrate for THCAS, CBDAS, and CBCAS. 3. PK shows >5-fold higher AP transcript levels than FN without copy-number difference, suggesting expression variation may underlie cannabinoid abundance differences.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to increase total cannabinoid levels by boosting precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Multiple >100 bp indels in and around AP, including intronic indels, may affect transcription or splicing

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hexanoic acid to hexanoyl-CoA for upstream cannabinoid precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive specialized metabolites with medicinal and recreational uses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Genome contains one copy of AAE1, supporting cannabinoid pathway gene inventory
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The new map identified one copy of AAE1 in the cannabis genome. 2. AAE1 is part of the known cannabinoid biosynthetic enzyme set. 3. No direct functional variation affecting metabolite level was tested in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Background pathway component for future engineering of cannabinoid precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because explicitly examined in genomic organization of cannabinoid pathway genes, but not a primary experimental focus for trait causality

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Polyketide branch leading to olivetolic/olivetol-related precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Tetraketide synthase involved in formation of the polyketide precursor of cannabinoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive specialized metabolites with medicinal and recreational uses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Genome contains two tandem copies of OLS, refining cannabinoid pathway gene organization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The map identified two tandem copies of olivetol synthase/tetraketide synthase. 2. OLS belongs to the upstream cannabinoid biosynthetic gene set. 3. This study did not directly test OLS allelic effects on cannabinoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Background pathway component for future pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because explicitly examined in genomic organization of cannabinoid pathway genes, but not a primary experimental focus for trait causality
